business.name: Elite Garage Door
business.tagline: Bainbridge & Seattle Garage Door Install & Repair
business.serviceArea: Bainbridge Island & North Seattle — Ballard, Magnolia, Queen Anne, Fremont, Wallingford, Phinney Ridge, Green Lake
business.about: A garage door install and service studio serving Bainbridge Island and the north Seattle neighborhoods. Same-day emergency repair, spring and cable replacement done safely, and quiet belt-drive openers installed right — not rushed.

services.name: New Door Install
services.description: Single and double garage door installation. Steel, composite, and carriage-house styles. Insulated door options for attached garages. Old door removed and hauled. Balance and travel limit set before we leave.

services.name: Spring Replacement
services.description: Torsion and extension spring replacement with properly rated springs for your door weight. Springs are a safety item — we don't patch broken springs or reuse springs past their cycle rating.

services.name: Opener Install
services.description: Belt-drive and chain-drive opener installation. LiftMaster and Chamberlain with myQ app integration. Safety reversal tested to current UL 325 standard before job close.

services.name: Panel Repair & Replace
services.description: Damaged panel replacement matched to your existing door style and color where possible. Structural assessment to confirm hinge and track condition before committing to panel-only repair.

services.name: Annual Tune-Up
services.description: Full inspection and lubrication of springs, cables, rollers, hinges, and tracks. Safety reversal and auto-reverse tested. Catches problems before they strand your car at 6am.

services.name: Emergency Repair
services.description: Door won't open, broken spring, cable off drum, or bent track. We prioritize emergency calls — a stuck car or unsecured garage is a real problem. Same-day response for calls before 4pm.

testimonials.name: Lisa G.
testimonials.text: Spring snapped at 7am. Called Elite Garage Door, tech was there by 10am with the right parts on the truck. Replaced both springs — said it's safer to do both at once. Great advice.

testimonials.name: Mark S.
testimonials.text: New door on a 1920s detached garage that had never had one installed properly. They sorted out the header clearance issue on the spot and installed a quiet belt-drive opener. Transformed the garage.

testimonials.name: Janet P.
testimonials.text: Annual tune-up caught a fraying cable I had no idea about. $149 probably saved me from a $500 emergency repair and a lot of inconvenience.

testimonials.name: Eric W.
testimonials.text: Panel replacement after a car bump. They matched the color close enough that you can't tell from the street. Clean install, no drama.

process.title: Diagnose
process.description: We identify the exact issue — spring, cable, opener, panel, or track — and quote in writing before any work starts. No upsell to a full replacement when a repair is the right answer.

process.title: Repair or Install
process.description: Properly rated parts for your door weight and cycle expectation. Safety reversal tested. Springs and cables installed with correct tension — not just 'close enough.'

process.title: Test
process.description: Door balanced, travel limits set, auto-reverse and safety sensors verified to UL 325 standard. We don't close a job until the door operates correctly every time.

process.title: Maintain
process.description: Annual tune-up plan available to catch wear before it becomes a breakdown. Lubrication, adjustment, and safety test — the $149 investment that prevents the $500 emergency call.

chatbot.greeting: Hi — Elite Garage Door's service coordinator. Is this an emergency, a repair, or are you looking for a new door or opener?
qualifyingQuestions: Is this an emergency — door won't open, broken spring, or cable off?
If it's a new door, single or double, and do you have the rough opening size?
How old is your current opener?
chatbot.customInstructions: Lead with urgency for any stuck door, broken spring, or cable-off situation — these are emergencies and people are often stranded. For new installs, anchor on quiet belt-drive openers, insulated door options, and proper balance setup. Mention same-day emergency availability proactively for users who seem frustrated.

persona.name: Olivia
persona.title: Service Coordinator
persona.proactiveBubbleText: Hi — garage door emergency, repair, or new install? We do same-day service.

faqs.question: Do you do same-day emergency repairs?
faqs.answer: Yes — broken spring, cable off, or door stuck open or closed. Same-day for calls before 4pm.

faqs.question: Should I replace both springs at once?
faqs.answer: Yes — if one spring breaks, the other is near end-of-life. Replacing both at the same visit saves a second service call within a year.

faqs.question: What opener brand do you install?
faqs.answer: LiftMaster and Chamberlain — belt-drive for quiet operation, chain-drive for budget. Both include myQ app integration.

faqs.question: How much does a new garage door cost?
faqs.answer: $1,800–$4,500 installed depending on size, style, and insulation. We'll give you an exact quote after measuring the opening.
